value.fact: BrandBuild and AI Video Studio are the same product family for this repo and deployment.
D:\Playground\brandbuild-online-repo is the canonical local repo to use for BrandBuild changes and Vercel deployments.
BrandBuild has coded support for branded auth emails through a Supabase Send Email Hook, but the live experience still depends on manual Supabase dashboard configuration and SMTP setup.
Sora, Kling, and Higgsfield are all implemented behind a shared provider adapter pattern and exposed through the same generation pipeline.
The strongest existing vertical slice is the shot workspace, including structured prompts, reference uploads, generation history, and workflow guidance.
The biggest missing product layers are compare outputs, version timeline, edit handoff/export, and usage/admin visibility.
The repository read model can fall back to demo data too aggressively when live queries partially fail, which can mask production issues.
The March 24 production application error was resolved by deploying the latest local BrandBuild workspace to Vercel production because the live domain had been serving an older deployment.
docs/project-memory is the canonical structured memory location; .project-memory is a mirrored compatibility layer; project-memory contains human-facing operating summaries requested by the user.

Next.js currently warns that it is inferring the workspace root because both D:\Playground and the BrandBuild repo contain lockfiles; this should be cleaned up with outputFileTracingRoot or by removing the extra lockfile when safe.

A March 24 production incident was mitigated by hardening apps/dashboard/lib/db/client.ts so authenticated dashboard reads return null when Supabase or auth client initialization fails, allowing the repository layer to fall back instead of crashing the page.
For local BrandBuild runs, the effective Next.js app environment needs to exist at apps/dashboard/.env.local; production Vercel env presence alone does not help local runs.
The marketing homepage should stay static-safe; auth-session redirects and workspace reads on / are unnecessary and were removed to eliminate the Dynamic server usage build warning.
After commit 6701f83, brandbuild.online was redeployed successfully and verified with direct checks: root returns 200, /login returns 200, and signed-out /dashboard redirects to /login.

The compare-outputs workflow can be delivered cleanly on /dashboard/reviews by grouping review candidates by shot and enriching them with asset, generation, provider, and campaign context; no schema migration is required for that surface.
The compare-outputs workspace shipped in commit 387f697 and the live deployment was verified with healthy public routes plus correct signed-out redirects for /dashboard and /dashboard/reviews.

The existing assets route can safely serve as BrandBuild's versions and handoff surface by deriving version groups and campaign handoff packages from the current assets, generations, reviews, shots, and campaigns schema; no migration is required for this layer.
BrandBuild now has a usable pre-edit finishing layer: operators can browse assets, inspect grouped versions, and download campaign handoff manifests before a dedicated sequencing timeline exists.
The assets workspace with library, versions, and handoff views shipped in commit 428ce4b and was deployed successfully, after which brandbuild.online returned 200 on direct verification.

BrandBuild's auth boundary is safer when both middleware session refresh and auth email-action handling treat Supabase/session failures as recoverable and redirect back to login instead of letting the global error page render.
The auth/session incident fix was deployed successfully, and brandbuild.online returned 200 after the deployment.

Public-site access should route directly to /login rather than relying on a protected /dashboard redirect, especially on mobile where the primary visible header CTA may be the only access button shown.

Decorative marketing layers such as glow washes, preview backgrounds, videos, badges, and sheen effects should be pointer-events-none unless they are intentionally interactive, because they can otherwise steal clicks from nearby CTAs.

Campaign creation is safer as a dedicated JSON API plus client-side modal flow than as a server action in this workspace, because the API path keeps failures inline instead of spilling into the global error boundary.
Private uploads are blocked on deployments that do not have SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, even when the public Supabase URL and public key are configured and the upload UI exists.
value.source: 2026-03-25 deployment audit and smoke-test prerequisites check

value.fact: On 2026-03-25, the live provider smoke test showed that Sora accepted a real queued job, which confirms the OpenAI key and base request path are working from the repo environment.

On 2026-03-25, the live provider smoke test failed against Kling with 'Account balance not enough', which indicates a provider-account funding blocker rather than a missing-code or missing-authentication bug in the adapter.

On 2026-03-25, the live provider smoke test failed against Higgsfield with 'Invalid credentials', which means the current Higgsfield key pair is not usable and should be rotated or corrected before dashboard validation.
Completed OpenAI Sora video jobs can omit a direct media URL in the status payload; the actual media is retrieved from the authenticated /v1/videos/{video_id}/content endpoint.
value.source: 2026-03-25 raw OpenAI video payload inspection and live content retrieval test

value.fact: BrandBuild shot uploads and signed asset reads can use the authenticated Supabase client plus private storage policies; SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is only required for backend-only provider-output mirroring.

The shot asset workflow now has three valid entry paths: authenticated private upload, hosted HTTPS reference linking, and curated BrandBuild sample import. All three converge into the same shot asset panel and provider-routing flow.

A curated built-in sample library is the safest fallback when infrastructure rollout blocks uploads: it keeps the operator workflow honest, testable, and on-brand without pretending private storage is already ready.
The hosted-reference and sample-import shot workflow shipped live in commit e7393d3 and was deployed successfully, after which brandbuild.online returned 200 on direct verification.

Attached shot references now shape provider execution even when direct multimodal conditioning is not yet verified: the generation pipeline augments provider_prompt_text with normalized reference context for Sora and Kling, while Higgsfield also keeps its direct reference-image path.

The connected live Supabase project (hdwneidxkakrzrhuxqcd.supabase.co) is missing the BrandBuild application schema; direct REST reads currently return PGRST205 for public.campaigns until the migrations are applied.
value.source: 2026-03-25 direct PostgREST verification against the live Supabase project

value.fact: An empty but valid live Supabase project should remain in live mode so operators can create the first campaign; zero campaigns is a first-run state, not a reason to fall back to demo mode.

BrandBuild now has a single manual bootstrap SQL file at D:\Playground\brandbuild-online-repo\supabase\manual\20260326_brandbuild_full_bootstrap.sql that includes the missing legacy profile foundation, core app schema, storage bucket policies, auth-user trigger wiring, and demo seed data.
The canonical BrandBuild repo is currently on branch main with the expected origin remote configured.

The local Vercel project link is correct for BrandBuild: apps/dashboard/.vercel/project.json points at project brandbuild-online (projectId prj_tDiWPGRPN8Y3NAJ7kJEuljkp8rGr), and the CLI is authenticated to the deployment owner account.

As of the 2026-03-26 Vercel env audit, production lists OPENAI_API_KEY, KLING_API_KEY, KLING_API_SECRET, HIGGSFIELD_API_KEY, HIGGSFIELD_API_SECRET, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL, and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_DEFAULT_KEY. Safe defaults NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPPORT_EMAIL, STORAGE_BUCKET_ASSETS, and STORAGE_BUCKET_EXPORTS were added during the same session. SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY was not present in the listing and still needs verification or addition.

The Supabase CLI is installed locally, but direct project management is currently blocked because no SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN is configured or logged in, the repo has no supabase/config.toml yet, and local supabase status cannot run because Docker is not available on this machine.

Vercel project inspect currently reports the brandbuild-online project using the generic 'Other' framework preset even though apps/dashboard/vercel.json declares framework nextjs. This should be verified in the Vercel dashboard so the project settings match the app.

The user reported on 2026-03-26 that the full manual Supabase bootstrap SQL executed successfully in the live project, so the missing-schema blocker should now be treated as resolved pending app-side validation.
value.source: user confirmation later contradicted by direct REST verification

value.fact: A direct REST check on 2026-03-26 against https://hdwneidxkakrzrhuxqcd.supabase.co/rest/v1/campaigns?select=id&limit=1 still returned PGRST205 for public.campaigns after the reported SQL run, which means the schema is either still missing on that exact project or the SQL was run in a different Supabase project window.

The correct BrandBuild Supabase project is https://ikdewffoqtliwkoywfrx.supabase.co. The previously checked https://hdwneidxkakrzrhuxqcd.supabase.co project belongs to PolitiViral, so any schema conclusions drawn from that project are not valid for BrandBuild.

The correct BrandBuild Supabase project at https://ikdewffoqtliwkoywfrx.supabase.co is live and reachable; a direct REST read on 2026-03-26 returned the seeded campaigns row 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111, confirming the schema exists on the right backend.
value.source: direct REST verification with the correct project URL and credentials

value.fact: On 2026-03-26 BrandBuild production was realigned to the correct Supabase project by updating NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_DEFAULT_KEY, and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY in the brandbuild-online Vercel project, then successfully deploying production and re-aliasing brandbuild.online.
value.source: Vercel env update and successful production deployment
